_comment: Machine-readable governor config for the Hermes autonomous loop system. GOVERNOR.md is the human/loop runbook; this file is the single source of truth for the numbers. Every loop skill MUST read this at the start of every cycle and obey it. Local-only working file (commit to origin; NEVER upstream).
_enabled: MASTER KILL SWITCH. While false, NO loop does any work — it logs 'governor disabled' and exits. Set true only after the governor is built + proven. Flip to false to pause everything instantly.
_shadow_mode: ACT MODE (2026-06-24, user: 'start the loop'). Action-taking loops may commit + transition, still gated by preflight + the evidence gate + the guard hook. Flip back to true to return to propose-only; kill switch (enabled:false) pauses everything.

caps._retries_per_stage: After the first failed attempt at a stage, at most ONE retry — and only with a genuinely DIFFERENT approach. A 2nd failure -> blocked:needs-human + escalate.
caps._max_build_verify_bounces: An issue may round-trip Verify->Build at most this many times, then freeze + escalate.
caps._min_cc_usage_headroom_pct: Do NOT start a cycle if `cc-usage` headroom is below this. Read via ~/bin/cc-usage.
caps._max_concurrent_action_loops: Read-only watchers (asc-poll, CI, digest) do NOT count toward this.

spin_detector._rule: If two consecutive iterations share a failure signature (same failing test / same error class / same diff), STOP immediately and escalate — do not even consume the retry. 'Never retry the same approach twice.'

evidence_gate._rule: A stage may NOT advance an issue to a passed/done state without a hard-evidence artifact attached to the Linear issue: a screenshot path, a log line, a DB row, an exit code, or a test result. No evidence -> no transition. Self-report is never accepted.

build._note: iOS builds run ONLY via scripts/ios-build.sh (machine-global mutex). A build with no swift-frontend progress past wedge_detect_seconds = SWBBuildService wedge -> alert + stop, NEVER kill -9 (recovery is logout/login).

heartbeat._rule: Each live loop writes a heartbeat each cycle (worklog comment + timestamp). Silence beyond this -> alert.

escalation._channels: User decision D2=C. Escalate the 5% / walls via BOTH a push notification AND a Linear assign+comment carrying a decision (tried A/B/C; here's the wall), not a debugging dump.

the_5_percent_never_autonomous: TestFlight ship / altool upload / asc-cloud trigger
any edit to stock NousResearch core (tui_gateway core, hermes_cli core, ui-tui, apps/desktop)
spending money / buying anything / provisioning paid infra
a direction fork the issue spec is silent on

merge_policy._comment: Decision (a), 2026-07-01: Abhi stepped OUT of the planning + merge gates for normal work, keeping only the safety gates (destructive / stock-core / ship / spend). The orchestrator MAY merge a PR to the shared branch autonomously, but ONLY when ALL FOUR conditions hold. Any PR failing any one condition escalates to Abhi instead of merging. This replaces the old blanket 'git merge is never autonomous' rule.
autonomous_merge_allowed_when_ALL: verified: the verifier posted hard green evidence (tests pass, exit 0) on the actual PR head
cross_provider_reviewed: at least one verdict-carrying reviewer of a DIFFERENT provider than the builder posted APPROVE (Opus-built -> GPT-5.5 reviewer; GPT/GLM-built -> Opus reviewer)
zero_fenced_paths: git diff --name-only touches NOTHING in forbidden_paths.block_globs (run scripts/loop-scope-check.sh)
revertible: the PR is a clean single-branch merge with no migration/irreversible side effect; `git revert` of the merge commit would cleanly undo it
merge_policy.on_any_condition_fail: Do NOT merge. Escalate to Abhi via the daily digest + a push notification, naming which condition failed and why.
merge_policy.post_merge: Log the merge in the daily digest. A merge is revertible, so Abhi's control is after-the-fact veto (revert), not pre-merge approval.
merge_policy.visibility_replaces_approval: Because Abhi no longer approves each wave/merge, a DAILY DIGEST to Telegram is mandatory: what was triaged, what is building, what merged, what is parked for Abhi. Abhi stays in command by seeing everything + veto-by-revert, not by gating each step.

forbidden_paths._comment: HARD scope fence (added 2026-07-01 after Slice B run: an engineer worker drifted from a plugin task into editing tui_gateway/server.py, a stock-core file. The edit was caught at review + contained in a throwaway worktree, but the worker should never have written it. These globs are a PRE-WRITE block: a worker MUST NOT create/modify any path matching these unless the card body explicitly names the path AND carries Abhi's approval. The verifier MUST reject any diff touching these paths that the card did not authorize.

enforcement.worker_prompt: Every dispatched card body MUST embed the SCOPE FENCE block (see card template). The worker treats block_globs as read-only: it may READ them for context but MUST NOT edit them. If the task appears to require such an edit, the worker STOPS and blocks with `needs-human`, naming the file — it does NOT edit and does NOT chase unrelated failing tests into fenced files.
enforcement.verifier_gate: The verifier runs `git diff --name-only` against the card's declared scope. ANY changed path matching block_globs that the card did not explicitly authorize = automatic REJECT, regardless of test results.
enforcement.rationale: Descriptive `autonomous_edits: blocked` in PROJECT.yaml was not enough — it was prose the worker never enforced. This is the machine-checkable fence.

concurrency_state._note: Loops register here on start and deregister on exit so the concurrency cap is enforceable. A stale entry past heartbeat silence = a crashed loop; reap + alert.

pipeline._comment: Buffered-basket pipeline rules (locked 2026-07-01). Full contract: docs/autonomous/wave-pipeline.md. These make the chain unbreakable: nothing Abhi owns can freeze the machine.
pipeline._ship_advances_pointer: RULE 1. The active_execution_wave pointer (PROJECT.yaml) advances to N+1 the MOMENT wave N uploads to TestFlight — NOT when Abhi device-verifies it. Verify is a separate async consumer reading the parking-lot buffer. A shipped-but-unverified wave never blocks the next wave's build.
pipeline._bump_blocked_slices: RULE 2. A slice that hits loop:blocked-human does NOT hold its wave hostage. Eject it into the next wave's candidate pool; close the current wave with its unblocked slices. The blocked task falls out of the moving basket into a side basket — the basket keeps rolling.
pipeline._pending_abhi_is_async: RULE 3. Every human gate (merge approval, device-verify, direction fork) writes to the pending-abhi parking lot he drains whenever. The orchestrator NEVER idles waiting on it — it returns to basket 0 and pulls the next thing.

wip_caps._shipped_unverified_builds: BACKPRESSURE. At this many builds shipped-but-not-device-verified, the SHIP stage THROTTLES (stops uploading new builds) but BUILD and PLAN keep running into their buffers. Draining a verify un-throttles ship automatically. Soft slowdown at one stage, never a chain break.
wip_caps._building_waves: At most this many waves in the 'building' stage at once while on the trust ladder (max_concurrent_action_loops still governs profiles within a wave).

roster._comment: Locked 2026-07-01 (Abhi). ONE model per profile, NO per-task branching — route to a different profile if a harder brain is needed. A profile is a full agent that JUDGES worker output (runs tests itself, accept/reject/retry, escalates), not a button-pusher.

spof_fallback._note: 3/6 profiles ride GPT-5.5. If the OpenAI lane is unavailable, Opus 4.8 is the NAMED fallback for verifier and reviewer so review capacity degrades gracefully instead of hard-stalling.
